speaker: customer
text: Hi! I saw Kaps' black and grey lion piece. How much would a half sleeve like that run?

speaker: studio
text: Hey Maya! Kaps' full-day sessions are $1,200 — a half sleeve is usually 2 sessions.

speaker: customer
text: Oof, that's more than I expected. Let me think about it.

speaker: customer
text: Is Kaps taking bookings for March? I want a realism piece of my grandfather's portrait.

speaker: studio
text: March is fully booked, first opening is mid-May. Want me to hold a consult?

speaker: customer
text: May is too far out, I wanted it before his anniversary in April. I'll check elsewhere.

speaker: customer
text: Do you do fine line florals? Thinking peonies on my forearm.

speaker: studio
text: Kaps specializes in black & grey realism — for fine line florals our artist Noa is amazing. Want to see her book?

speaker: customer
text: I really wanted Kaps specifically after seeing his work at the convention.

speaker: studio
text: Kaps can absolutely do a botanical piece in his style — softer realism. Want a consult?

speaker: customer
text: Maybe. I'll get back to you after payday.

speaker: customer
text: Hey I put down a deposit last year for a chest piece with Kaps but had to cancel. Is that deposit still good?

speaker: studio
text: Hi James — deposits are held for 12 months. Yours is still valid until August. Want to rebook?

speaker: customer
text: Work has been crazy. What weekend slots does he have?

speaker: studio
text: Kaps does Saturdays once a month — next open Saturday is in 6 weeks.

speaker: customer
text: Let me look at my schedule and circle back.

speaker: customer
text: Your studio is in Vegas right? I'm in LA — is it worth flying out for a session with Kaps?

speaker: studio
text: We're Las Vegas based, yes! Lots of clients fly in — full-day sessions make the trip worth it.

speaker: customer
text: Hmm, flights plus hotel plus $1200... I need to budget for this. Do you ever do LA guest spots?

speaker: studio
text: Kaps does occasional guest spots — none scheduled right now but I can add you to the list.

speaker: customer
text: Yes please add me!

speaker: customer
text: Do you guys do payment plans? I want a full back piece but can't drop 4 grand at once.

speaker: studio
text: We do session-by-session pricing — each session is booked and paid separately.

speaker: customer
text: So I could space sessions out every couple months? That could work actually.

speaker: studio
text: Exactly. Want me to set up a consult with Kaps to plan the design?

speaker: customer
text: Not yet, saving up for the first session. Maybe in the summer.

speaker: customer
text: I messaged a few weeks ago about a memorial piece for my mom. Still thinking about the design.

speaker: studio
text: Take your time Sofia. When you're ready Kaps can sketch a few concepts from photos you share.

speaker: customer
text: Thank you. It's hard to decide on something so permanent about her.

speaker: customer
text: How long is the wait for a sleeve consult with Kaps?

speaker: studio
text: Consults are about 2 weeks out, the sleeve itself would start about 6 weeks after.

speaker: customer
text: Cool. And ballpark cost for a full sleeve, japanese style mixed with realism?

speaker: studio
text: Full sleeves run 4-6 full-day sessions at $1,200 each depending on detail.

speaker: customer
text: Ok that's a real investment. Let me talk to my wife lol

speaker: customer
text: Hi, I won a gift certificate at the charity event for a session with Kaps. How do I redeem it?

speaker: studio
text: Congrats Hannah! Just reply with 2-3 date options and the certificate number and we'll get you in.

speaker: customer
text: Yo is Kaps the one who did the chicano lettering piece on your page? That's exactly my style.

speaker: studio
text: That's him! He loves lettering + black and grey work. What are you thinking?

speaker: customer
text: Family name across the collarbone with roses. How much roughly?

speaker: studio
text: That's usually a single full-day session — $1,200 flat.

speaker: customer
text: Quote me after the holidays man, December is killing my wallet.

speaker: customer
text: First tattoo ever and I'm honestly nervous about the pain. Is a rib piece a bad idea for a first one?

speaker: studio
text: Ribs are one of the tougher spots — many first-timers start with forearm or shoulder. Kaps is great with nervous clients.

speaker: customer
text: That's reassuring. I'll think about placement and message back.

speaker: customer
text: I need a cover-up of an old tribal piece on my shoulder. Can Kaps handle that?

speaker: studio
text: Cover-ups are Kaps' bread and butter — send a photo of the current piece?

speaker: customer
text: Sent. It's pretty dark ink.

speaker: studio
text: He can work with that — likely a larger black & grey design over it. Consult is free.

speaker: customer
text: Booked it for Thursday. Looking forward.

speaker: customer
text: What's your deposit policy? I got burned by another studio that kept my deposit when THEY cancelled.

speaker: studio
text: Deposits here roll forward if we ever reschedule — you only lose it on a no-show. It's in writing.

speaker: customer
text: Ok that's fair. Still a bit gun-shy but I love Kaps' portfolio.

speaker: customer
text: My buddy and I both want pieces from the same flash set. Do you do group bookings?

speaker: studio
text: We can book you back-to-back on the same day with Kaps — makes a great day of it.

speaker: customer
text: Sweet. He gets paid biweekly so we'll aim for end of month.

speaker: customer
text: Does Kaps do color realism or only black and grey? I want a hummingbird with real vibrant colors.

speaker: studio
text: Kaps is primarily black & grey. For color realism, guest artist Rio visits quarterly — jaw-dropping color work.

speaker: customer
text: When is Rio next in town?

speaker: studio
text: Next visit isn't announced yet — want me to notify you?

speaker: customer
text: Yes! And send me Rio's page.

speaker: customer
text: Quick question — do you tattoo over scars? I have a surgical scar on my forearm I'd like to incorporate.

speaker: studio
text: Yes, once scars are fully healed (12+ months). Kaps has done several scar-integration pieces.

speaker: customer
text: It's been 3 years. What would a forearm piece incorporating it cost?

speaker: studio
text: Depends on size — likely one full-day session, $1,200. Free consult to assess it.

speaker: customer
text: stop

speaker: customer
text: I'm planning a matching piece with my sister for our mom's 60th. She lives in Phoenix though — can we book the same weekend?

speaker: studio
text: Absolutely — we can do you both on a Saturday. Kaps loves meaningful matching pieces.

speaker: customer
text: Perfect, I'll coordinate flights with her and email you dates.

speaker: customer
text: Saw the studio won best black and grey at the expo. Congrats. Is Kaps' books open or closed rn?

speaker: studio
text: Open! He books 6-8 weeks out currently.

speaker: customer
text: What do I need for a consult? References?

speaker: studio
text: Reference images + placement idea + rough budget. That's it.

speaker: customer
text: Your prices went up since last year didn't they? I was quoted $950 for a day session in 2025.

speaker: studio
text: Yes — Kaps' day rate is now $1,200 as of January. His waitlist doubled after the expo win.

speaker: customer
text: I get it, he's worth it, just stings. Might wait for a flash day or promo.

speaker: studio
text: We do occasional flash events — I'll make sure you hear about the next one.

speaker: customer
text: I keep chickening out lol. Third time messaging you guys about the same phoenix back piece idea.

speaker: studio
text: Ha! Third time's the charm Chris. Want to just do a no-pressure consult? Nothing gets inked at a consult.

speaker: customer
text: You know what, maybe. Let me get through tax season first.
